basics.name: Zachary Rothstein
basics.label: Career Transition – Software Engineering to Healthcare
basics.summary: Current ophthalmic technician intern with 2+ years as a software engineer and strong skills in documentation, client communication, and process improvement. Actively seeking clinical healthcare positions while completing certifications in EKG technology, phlebotomy, and medical assisting.

highlights: Minor: Religious Studies
Dean's List 2020-2022
Robert Maynard Hutchins Scholar 2020-2022

work.name: Eyenamics NY
highlights: Conduct patient intake interviews and document histories for 15-20 patients daily
Scribe physician notes in real time
Perform OCT scans, visual field tests, and color vision assessments
Process clinical orders through the EHR system

work.name: Dr. Harold Klestzick
highlights: Conducted dozens of ECGs in a cardiology clinical setting
Recorded clinical notes and observed patient interactions

work.name: NYP Columbia University Irving Medical Center
work.position: Patient Care Volunteer - Golden Spoons Program
highlights: Provide direct face-to-face patient support during mealtimes in neurology inpatient and ICU units
Assist patients in meeting nutrition goals
Coordinate with clinical staff to respond to individual patient needs

work.name: Crisis Text Line
highlights: Provide real-time text-based crisis intervention support utilizing active listening, collaborative problem-solving, and safety planning
Completed 15+ hours of supervised training in de-escalation and crisis management

work.name: Mechanical Orchard
highlights: Delivered real-time web applications and gRPC services supporting 20+ users
Architected framework solutions and owned CI/CD pipelines while migrating 50+ legacy batch jobs to modern cloud-native architectures
Transformed 50+ CICS mainframe screens into modern web interfaces
Regularly interfaced with client stakeholders to gather requirements and provide updates

work.name: Google Maps
highlights: Analyzed developer workflows for 3,000+ individuals and implemented QA testing fixes increasing developer velocity across the org
Collaborated on hybrid teams, writing clear documentation, engaging in regular meetings, and integrating teammate feedback

work.name: Google Ads
highlights: Automated UI troubleshooting notifications supporting 10,000+ users at the intersection of Ads and Search, driving high-impact improvements to revenue-critical workflows on a large cross-functional team
Debugged student code, clarified complex concepts during Office Hours and Discussion sections
Graded assignments for 100+ students

leadership.organization: Department of Computer Science, University of Chicago
highlights: Wrote code, documentation, and Jupyter Notebooks supporting research in Computer Security, Privacy, and Machine Learning
Implemented featurization for a research ML classifier
Worked with electronic health record (EHR) datasets, gaining hands-on familiarity with clinical data structures and healthcare data privacy considerations
